Items.Title: AZ：Git 备忘单 | A-Z: Git Cheat Sheet
Items.Description:
布拉克博杜罗格鲁

发表于 6 月 10 日

  • 它由 Linux 操作系统的创造者 Linus Torvalds 在 2005 年创建。
  • 它允许您查看更改历史、恢复到以前的版本以及管理不同的开发分支。

Repository:

  • 存储库，通常称为“repo”，是 Git 存储所有文件及其完整历史记录的中心位置。 它包含项目的所有版本和有关更改的元数据。

Commit:

  • 提交是项目在特定时间点的快照。 它表示对文件所做的一组更改。 每个提交都有一个唯一的标识符，它包括作者、时间戳和提交消息等信息。

Branch:

  • 分支是存储库中的一条单独的开发线。 它允许多个开发人员同时处理不同的功能或错误修复，而不会干扰彼此的代码。

Merge:

  • 合并是将更改从一个分支集成到另一个分支的过程。 它合并提交并解决更改重叠时出现的任何冲突。 这通常用于将功能分支中所做的更改带回主分支（通常称为“master”或“main”）。

Remote:

  • 远程是托管在服务器上的 Git 存储库的副本，允许开发人员之间进行协作。 流行的远程存储库托管服务包括 GitHub、GitLab 和 Bitbucket。

Pull and Push:

  • 拉取是指从远程存储库中检索最新的更改并将它们合并到本地存储库中。 推送意味着将您的本地更改发送到远程存储库，使它们可供其他协作者使用。

有用的 Git 命令：

  • 在当前目录中初始化一个新的 Git 存储库。 它创建一个新的 .git 子目录来存储版本控制信息。
  • 在本地计算机上创建远程存储库的副本。 它下载所有文件及其整个历史记录。
  • 向暂存区添加文件或更改。 这准备要提交的文件。
 git commit -m "<message>"
  • 将暂存区域的快照记录为存储库中的新提交。 提交消息描述了提交中所做的更改。
  • 显示存储库的当前状态。 它显示哪些文件被修改、暂存或未跟踪。
  • 显示当前更改与上次提交之间的差异。 它显示了修改文件中的逐行差异。
  • 列出存储库中的所有分支。 当前分支标有星号。
  • 切换到不同的分支。 它更新工作目录以匹配指定的分支。
  • 将指定分支合并到当前分支。 它集成了更改并创建了一个新的提交。
  • 从远程存储库中获取最新的更改并将它们合并到当前分支中。 它通常用于使用其他人所做的更改来更新本地存储库。
  • 将本地提交推送到远程存储库。 它使用您的更改更新远程存储库，使它们可供其他人使用。
  • 显示存储库中的提交历史记录。 它显示提交消息、作者、时间戳和提交标识符。
 git remote add <name> <url>
  • 将远程存储库与名称和 URL 相关联。 它允许您通过给定名称引用远程存储库。
  • 列出与当前存储库关联的所有远程存储库及其 URL。
  • 临时保存尚未准备好提交的更改。 它允许您在不提交或放弃更改的情况下切换分支。
  • 检索最近隐藏的更改并将它们应用于当前分支。 一旦应用更改，它就会从存储中删除更改。
  • 它通过创建包含相反更改的新提交来撤消先前的提交。 它允许您安全地撤消更改而无需重写历史记录。

在GitHub上关注我👾
感谢您的阅读。🙋

一旦暂停，burakboduroglu 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，burakboduroglu 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，burakboduroglu 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 burakboduroglu 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 burakboduroglu 的方法：

降低 burakboduroglu 发布的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 burakboduroglu 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Burak Boduroğlu

Posted on Jun 10

 
  • Git is a distributed version control system widely used in software development.
  • It was created by Linus Torvalds, the creator of the Linux operating system, in 2005.
  • Git allows multiple developers to collaborate on a project by keeping track of changes made to files and coordinating the merging of those changes.

Version Control:

  • Git is primarily used for version control, which means it keeps track of changes made to files over time. It allows you to view the history of changes, revert to previous versions, and manage different branches of development.

Repository:

  • A repository, often referred to as a "repo," is a central location where Git stores all the files and their complete history. It contains all the versions of the project and metadata about the changes.

Commit:

  • A commit is a snapshot of the project at a specific point in time. It represents a set of changes made to the files. Each commit has a unique identifier, and it includes information such as the author, timestamp, and a commit message.

Branch:

  • A branch is a separate line of development within a repository. It allows multiple developers to work on different features or bug fixes simultaneously without interfering with each other's code.

Merge:

  • Merging is the process of integrating changes from one branch into another. It combines the commits and resolves any conflicts that arise when changes overlap. This is often used to bring the changes made in a feature branch back into the master branch (usually called "master" or "main").

Remote:

  • A remote is a copy of a Git repository that is hosted on a server, allowing collaboration between developers. Popular remote repository hosting services include GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.

Pull and Push:

  • Pulling refers to retrieving the latest changes from a remote repository and merging them into the local repository. Pushing means sending your local changes to the remote repository, making them available to other collaborators.

Useful Git Commands:

  • Initializes a new Git repository in the current directory. It creates a new .git subdirectory that stores the version control information.
  • Creates a copy of a remote repository on your local machine. It downloads all the files and their entire history.
  • Adds a file or changes to the staging area. This prepares the file to be committed. 
git commit -m "<message>"
  • Records a snapshot of the staging area as a new commit in the repository. The commit message describes the changes made in the commit.
  • Shows the current status of the repository. It displays which files are modified, staged, or untracked.
  • Displays the differences between the current changes and the last commit. It shows the line-by-line differences in modified files.
  • Lists all the branches in the repository. The current branch is marked with an asterisk.
  • Switches to a different branch. It updates the working directory to match the specified branch.
  • Merges the specified branch into the current branch. It integrates the changes and creates a new commit.
  • Fetches the latest changes from a remote repository and merges them into the current branch. It is often used to update the local repository with changes made by others.
  • Pushes local commits to a remote repository. It updates the remote repository with your changes, making them available to others.
  • Shows a history of commits in the repository. It displays commit messages, authors, timestamps, and commit identifiers. 
git remote add <name> <url>
  • Associates a remote repository with a name and URL. It allows you to refer to the remote repository by the given name.
  • Lists all the remote repositories associated with the current repository along with their URLs.
  • Temporarily saves changes that are not ready to be committed. It allows you to switch branches without committing or discarding the changes.
  • Retrieves the most recently stashed changes and applies them to the current branch. It removes the changes from the stash once they have been applied.
  • It undoes a previous commit by creating a new commit that contains the opposite changes. It allows you to safely undo changes without rewriting history.

Follow me on GitHub👾
Thank you for reading.🙋

Mwendwa Bundi Emma - Jun 8

Items.Title: React 入门：释放 Web 开发的力量 | Getting Started with React: Unleashing the Power of Web Development
Items.Description:
在不断发展的 Web 开发世界中，React 已经确立了自己作为游戏规则改变者的地位，彻底改变了我们构建用户界面和交互式 Web 应用程序的方式。 今年年初，我致力于在 React 中获得深入的知识和实践经验，我很高兴与您分享我在使用这个强大的 JavaScript 库时熟悉的基本概念。 我们将一起探索其不可思议的潜力，并揭示塑造我的旅程的核心原则。

了解反应：
它使开发人员能够创建可重用的 UI 组件，这些组件可以根据数据的变化有效地更新和呈现，从而产生动态和交互式的 Web 应用程序。

基于组件的架构：
组件是可重用的构建块，封装了自己的逻辑和渲染，使它们高度模块化和灵活。 通过将复杂的 UI 分解为更小的、易于管理的组件，React 提高了代码的可重用性、可维护性和可扩展性。

状态和道具：
在 React 中，state 和 props 是必不可少的概念。 通过管理状态和传递道具，React 可以创建交互式和数据驱动的 UI。 使用 useState 和 useEffect 挂钩，管理状态和处理副作用更加直观和高效。

虚拟 DOM：优化性能：
通过使用 diffing 算法，React 仅有效地更新 DOM 的必要部分，最大限度地减少不必要的重新渲染并提高性能。 这种方法允许 React 提供闪电般快速的用户体验，即使是复杂和数据密集型应用程序。

参加聚会和会议还可以提供交流和了解最新趋势和最佳实践的机会。

结论：凭借其基于组件的架构、JSX 语法、通过虚拟 DOM 的高效渲染以及繁荣的生态系统，我可以理解为什么 React 已将自己确立为现代 Web 开发的强大引擎。 有了对基础知识的扎实理解，您就可以很好地释放 React 的全部潜力，并创建引人入胜、动态且高性能的 Web 应用程序。 因此，深入研究、探索并让 React 助力您作为 Web 开发人员的旅程。

请记住，旅程不会在这里结束。 随着您继续深入研究 React，可能性是无穷无尽的。 编码愉快！



原文:

In the ever-evolving world of web development, React has established itself as a game-changer, revolutionising the way we construct user interfaces and interactive web applications. Having dedicated the beginning of this year to gaining intensive knowledge and hands-on experience in React, I am thrilled to share with you the fundamental concepts I have familiarised myself with while working with this powerful JavaScript library. Together, we will explore its incredible potential and uncover the core principles that have shaped my journey.

Understanding React:
At its core, React is a declarative and efficient JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It enables developers to create reusable UI components that efficiently update and render based on changes in data, resulting in dynamic and interactive web applications.

The Component-Based Architecture:
React's strength lies in its component-based architecture. Components are reusable building blocks that encapsulate their own logic and rendering, making them highly modular and flexible. By breaking down complex UIs into smaller, manageable components, React promotes code reusability, maintainability, and scalability.

JSX: A Powerful Combination of JavaScript and HTML:
React introduces JSX (JavaScript XML), a syntax extension that allows you to write HTML-like code directly within your JavaScript files. This seamless integration of JavaScript and HTML enables the creation of dynamic and expressive UI components, making React a joy to work with.

State and Props:
In React, state and props are essential concepts. State represents the internal data of a component, while props are the properties passed from a parent component to its children. By managing state and passing props, React enables the creation of interactive and data-driven UIs. With the useState and useEffect hooks, managing state and handling side effects is even more intuitive and efficient.

Virtual DOM: Optimising Performance:
React's Virtual DOM (Document Object Model) is a lightweight representation of the actual DOM. By using a diffing algorithm, React efficiently updates only the necessary parts of the DOM, minimising unnecessary re-rendering and improving performance. This approach allows React to deliver lightning-fast user experiences, even with complex and data-intensive applications.

React Community and Learning Resources:
The React community is thriving, providing abundant resources for learning and support. Online tutorials, documentation, and vibrant developer communities on platforms like Stack Overflow and Reddit offer invaluable insights and assistance. Attending meet-ups and conferences can also provide opportunities for networking and staying up to date with the latest trends and best practices.

Conclusion: With its component-based architecture, JSX syntax, efficient rendering through the Virtual DOM, and a thriving ecosystem, I can see why React has established itself as a powerhouse for modern web development. Armed with a solid understanding of the basics, you can be well on your way to unleashing the full potential of React and creating engaging, dynamic, and performant web applications. So, dive in, explore, and let React empower your journey as a web developer.

Remember, the journey doesn't end here. As you continue to dive deeper into React, the possibilities are endless. Happy coding!
Items.Author: keenangee

Items.Title: CodeAtlas.xyz 正在 Product Hunt 上发布 | CodeAtlas.xyz is launching on Product Hunt
Items.Description:
代码图集

发表于 6 月 10 日

您是软件开发人员吗？ 让下周对您和您的团队来说更加轻松！

单击以获取通知并查看→
一旦暂停，codeatlasdotxyz 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，codeatlasdotxyz 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codeatlasdotxyz 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codeatlasdotxyz 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codeatlasdotxyz：

降低 codeatlasdotxyz 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codeatlasdotxyz 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

CodeAtlas

Posted on Jun 10

Are you a software developer? Make next week so much easier for you and your team!

Launching our early access to CodeAtlas.xyz on Product Hunt on Monday 8am BST

Click to be notified and check it out →
producthunt.com/products/codeatlas-xyz

Brian Bethencourt - Jun 9

Once suspended, codeatlasdotxyz will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codeatlasdotxyz will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codeatlasdotxyz will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codeatlasdotxyz is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codeatlasdotxyz:

Make all posts by codeatlasdotxyz less visible

codeatlasdotxyz consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codeatlasdotxyz will restore default visibility to their posts.
